articles.title: FLASHLIST V2: A GROUND-UP REWRITE FOR REACT NATIVE'S NEW ARCHITECTURE
articles.summary: FlashList v2 is a complete rewrite of the popular React Native list component that removes the need for item size estimates and has faster load times with improved scrolling performance. The new version uses React Native's New Architecture to synchronously measure layouts and make corrections before rendering, removing the dependency on native code that caused issues in v1. Some of the improvements include pixel-perfect scrolling precision, better horizontal list support, adaptive rendering algorithms, and better masonry layouts.

articles.title: UNLOCKING WEB WORKERS WITH REACT: A STEP-BY-STEP GUIDE
articles.summary: This article is a step-by-step guide on how to implement Web Workers in React apps to prevent UI freezing during heavy computational tasks. It teaches this through the progression from a basic problem (calculating Fibonacci numbers blocking the main thread) to more complex solutions. It goes over using Web Workers for background processing, implementing task queues for ordered execution, and using Shared Workers to share state across browser tabs.

articles.title: GO'S RACE DETECTOR HAS A MUTEX BLIND SPOT
articles.summary: Go's race detector has a blind spot when detecting data races involving mutex-protected code. While the detector can identify races that don't involve synchronization primitives regardless of timing, it may miss races in mutex-protected sections depending on the execution order. Despite this limitation, Go's race detector is one of the best tools available for dynamic race detection to keep code relatively race-free.

articles.title: WE REPLACED PASSWORDS WITH SOMETHING WORSE
articles.summary: The increasingly common login method of using a 6-digit code sent to an email or phone number isn't a good thing. It's bad for account security because it makes users vulnerable to attacks where they unknowingly enter codes prompted by attackers. Password managers cannot protect against this type of phishing, leaving users exposed.

articles.title: A NEW WORST CODER HAS ENTERED THE CHAT: VIBE CODING WITHOUT CODE KNOWLEDGE
articles.summary: While vibe coding makes creating a simple app easy, the resulting code is often messy, insecure, and requires a lot of troubleshooting from AI and real devs. On the other hand, devs can use AI to their advantage to speed up their learning.

articles.title: GPT-5
articles.summary: OpenAI's GPT-5 is its smartest and fastest model yet. It is now available to everyone in ChatGPT. GPT-5 has better intelligence for math, science, finance, law, and more, with more useful and accurate responses and deeper thinking capabilities. It also comes with more customization options, voice improvements, study mode, and connector integration with Gmail and Google Calendar.

articles.title: CURSOR CLI BETA BUILT TO HELP YOU SHIP, RIGHT FROM YOUR TERMINAL
articles.summary: Cursor CLI is a tool designed to help users plan, search, and build code directly from their terminal, integrating with various IDEs and terminals. It provides full control, allowing users to review agent edits, steer in real-time, and set custom rules.

articles.title: MEET YOUR NEW AI CODING TEAMMATE: GEMINI CLI GITHUB ACTIONS
articles.summary: Gemini CLI GitHub Actions is a free AI coding teammate now available in beta. Designed for the automation of routine coding tasks within GitHub repositories, it is triggered by events like new issues or pull requests. It offers three initial open-source workflows: intelligent issue triage, accelerated pull request reviews, and on-demand collaboration through the "@gemini-cli" tag.

articles.title: BUILDING BLUESKY COMMENTS FOR MY BLOG
articles.summary: Existing blog comment systems like Disqus and self-hosted solutions annoyed this dev due to performance issues, maintenance overhead, and lack of ownership. They decided to use Bluesky as a comment system, using its decentralized nature, rich content support, real identities, and cross-platform conversations. The implementation involved fetching comments from Bluesky using the AT Protocol and rendering them in a nested structure within their Astro blog.

articles.title: ADDRESS FORMATS AROUND THE WORLD
articles.summary: Address formats vary a lot around the world, with differences in element order (house numbers before or after street names), numbering systems (sequential vs. distance-based), and postal code formats (ranging from 4-8 characters with various patterns). These variations create challenges for web devs and form designers, who must accommodate different cultural expectations and address structures rather than assuming a single "standard" format.

articles.title: PUBLISHERS OPPOSE W3C PLAN TO KILL THIRD-PARTY COOKIES
articles.summary: The Movement for an Open Web (MOW), a web publishers' advocacy group, has filed a complaint with the UK's Competition and Markets Authority challenging the World Wide Web Consortium's (W3C) call to eliminate third-party cookies. MOW argues that the W3C's position is anti-competitive and favors Google over smaller publishers, who rely on third-party cookie data since they don't have access to the large first-party data sources available to tech giants like Google, Apple, and Meta. The complaint asks the CMA to pressure the W3C to withdraw its announcement supporting the removal of third-party cookies, claiming it violates antitrust obligations and Google's previous commitments to involve competitors in privacy-related design processes.

articles.title: AI ELEMENTS
articles.summary: AI Elements is a new component library built on shadcn/ui for building AI-native applications.

articles.title: GITHUB PROFILE HEADER GENERATOR
articles.summary: A tool to generate a customized header image for your GitHub profile or project README.

articles.title: HMPL
articles.summary: HMPL is a small, server-oriented JavaScript templating language that uses customizable fetch requests, JSON5, and DOMPurify to render UI efficiently, reduce client-side file sizes, and offer an alternative to tools like HTMX and Alpine.js.

articles.title: NOTTE
articles.summary: Notte is a full-stack framework that combines AI with traditional scripting for reliable automation to help developers build and deploy AI agents that interact with the web.
